name: Vitebsk Region (Belarus)

colors.symbolism: the golden wreath and mural crown

description: A green field bearing the coat of arms of Vitebsk Region: a red shield charged with the Pahonia (a mounted knight in silver), ensigned with a golden mural crown and framed by golden oak and blue ribbons.
history: Vitebsk Region (Vitsebsk Voblast) is the northern region of Belarus, bordering Russia, Latvia and Lithuania and centred on Vitebsk. Its flag places the regional arms on a green field and was adopted in 2009.

features.role: Arms of Vitebsk Region - a red shield with the Pahonia knight
